title: Sea Turtle Rescue Assistance and Rehabilitation Act of 2025
short_title: Sea Turtle Rescue Assistance and Rehabilitation Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill creates a new government grant program to help save sea turtles that are sick, hurt, or trapped. The grants will pay for rescuing sea turtles, taking care of them until they get better, and doing research to learn more about sea turtle health. The bill also sets up a special emergency fund that the government can use quickly when sea turtles need urgent help. This new program works just like an existing program that already helps rescue marine mammals like whales and dolphins.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Sea Turtle Rescue Assistance and Rehabilitation Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Sea Turtle Rescue, Rehabilitation, and Response
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Marine Mammal Protection Act of nineteen seventy two. It adds new rules about helping sea turtles. The Secretary of Commerce and the Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service can now give out special grants to help rescue and heal sea turtles. These grants work only if Congress provides money specifically for sea turtle rescue work. Organizations that want these grants must follow the same rules that already exist for marine mammal grants. They also must have proper permits from the government to work with endangered sea turtles. These permits come from either the Endangered Species Act or special agreements with the government. Organizations must follow government rules for taking care of sea turtles in captivity when they use facilities for this purpose. They must also report data about sea turtle strandings as required by the Sea Turtle Stranding and Salvage Network. The government can only give out these sea turtle grants for seven years after this law starts. This section also creates two separate funds in the United States Treasury. One fund is called the Joseph R. Geraci Marine Mammal Rescue and Rapid Response Fund. The other fund is called the Sea Turtle Rescue, Rehabilitation, and Rapid Response Fund. Both funds earn interest while the money sits there. The Secretary can only use money from these funds to provide emergency help for marine mammals and sea turtles.
